facts: Venus, the hottest planet in our solar system, has a surface temperature of 462°C (864°F).
But meet exoplanet WASP-12b, orbiting a star 1400 light-years away. Its dayside reaches an incredible 2500°C (4530°F)! Hotter than many stars!
WASP-12b is tidally locked and so close to its star, it's distorted into an egg-shape by immense gravitational forces.
idea.payoff: This 'hot Jupiter' exoplanet is literally being ripped apart and devoured by its own star!
idea.title: Planet of Fire! 🔥🤯

scenes.visual: A visually striking, fiery representation of Venus's surface, with lava flows and extreme heat haze.
A close-up of Venus, subtly showing a temperature gauge rising. Text appears clearly over the planet image.

scenes.text: Meet WASP-12b: Dayside hits 2500°C! Hotter than stars!
scenes.visual: A vibrant, extremely hot exoplanet (WASP-12b) with a visible, glowing atmosphere, orbiting a much larger, brighter star in the background. The exoplanet is intensely bright on its dayside.

scenes.text: Tidally locked & egg-shaped by star's gravity.
scenes.visual: A dynamic shot showing WASP-12b clearly distorted into an egg-shape due to extreme proximity and gravitational forces from its nearby star. Gravitational pull lines can be subtly visualized.

scenes.text: WASP-12b: RIPPED APART & DEVOURED by its star!
scenes.visual: A dramatic, intense visual of WASP-12b actively being stripped of its material, with streams of glowing gas spiraling into its host star. A truly destructive and impactful scene.
